mappings.rationale: Directly covers the geography and history of Southeast Asia, relevant to the entire section on European imperialism in the region from Myanmar to the Philippines.
Relevant to British colonial rule in Burma and Malaya, the Anglo-Burmese Wars, and British establishment of power over the Malay sultanates discussed in subsection A.1.
Relevant to the multiple European imperial enterprises in Southeast Asia including British, French, Dutch, Spanish, and Portuguese colonialism covered throughout the section.
Relevant to the effects of European colonialism on Southeast Asian societies, the destruction of traditional institutions, and the rise of nationalism discussed throughout.
Relevant to the nascent independence movements emerging across Southeast Asia by the end of the period covered, including the Philippine Revolution and Vietnamese nationalism.
Relevant to the rise of nationalist movements in the Philippines (19th century) and other Southeast Asian nations under European colonial rule.
Relevant to the Anglo-French activity in Southeast Asia, territorial acquisitions, and the diplomatic maneuvering among European powers discussed in subsections A.2-3.
Relevant to the role of Theravada Buddhism in Siam and the destruction of the traditional church-state relationship in Burma under British colonialism discussed in subsections A.1 and A.3.
Relevant to the Islamic societies of the Indonesian Archipelago and the Malay sultanates discussed in subsections A.1.b and B.2.
Relevant to the commercial enterprises of the Dutch East India Company, the Culture System in Java, and the colonial economic exploitation discussed in subsections B.1-2.
Relevant to Portuguese and Spanish exploration and commercial activities in Southeast Asia discussed in subsection B.1.a.
